content.problemStatement:
You are given three positive integers N,A,N, A,N,A, and BBB. Further, it is known that ‚ą£A‚ąíB‚ą£‚Č§1|A-B| \leq 1‚ą£A‚ąíB‚ą£‚Č§1.

Count the number of integers XXX such that:


1‚Č§X‚Č§N1 \leq X \leq N1‚Č§X‚Č§N; and
There exist **non-negative integers** ppp and qqq satisfying the equation
A‚čÖp+B‚čÖq=XA\cdot p + B\cdot q = XA‚čÖp+B‚čÖq=X
Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case will contain three space-separated integers N,A,N, A,N,A, and BBB, as described in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of integers XXX satisfying the given condition.

Explanation:
Test case 111: With A=3,B=4,A = 3, B = 4,A=3,B=4, and N=7N = 7N=7, we have the following valid XXX:

X=3=1‚čÖ3+0‚čÖ4X = 3 = 1\cdot 3 + 0\cdot 4X=3=1‚čÖ3+0‚čÖ4
X=4=0‚čÖ3+1‚čÖ4X = 4 = 0\cdot 3 + 1\cdot 4X=4=0‚čÖ3+1‚čÖ4
X=6=2‚čÖ3+0‚čÖ4X = 6 = 2\cdot 3 + 0\cdot 4X=6=2‚čÖ3+0‚čÖ4
X=7=1‚čÖ3+1‚čÖ4X = 7 = 1\cdot 3 + 1\cdot 4X=7=1‚čÖ3+1‚čÖ4

Test case 222: For A=B=3A = B = 3A=B=3 and N=7N = 7N=7, only 3=1‚čÖ3+0‚čÖ33 = 1\cdot 3 + 0\cdot 33=1‚čÖ3+0‚čÖ3 and 6=1‚čÖ3+1‚čÖ36 = 1\cdot 3 + 1\cdot 36=1‚čÖ3+1‚čÖ3 are valid.
